title: Windows Forms entry points should be marked with STAThread
description: Windows Forms entry points should be marked with STAThreadAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugwinformspitfallWhy is this an issue?When an assembly uses Windows Forms (classes and interfaces from theSystem.Windows.Formsnamespace) its entry point should be marked with theSTAThreadAttributeto indicate that the threading model should be "Single-Threaded Apartment" (STA) which is the only one supported by Windows Forms.This rule raises an issue when the entry point (static void Mainmethod) of an assembly using Windows Forms is not marked as STA.Noncompliant code exampleusing System; using System.Windows.Forms; namespace MyLibrary { public class MyForm: Form { public MyForm() { this.Text = "Hello World!"; } public static void Main() // Noncompliant { var form = new MyForm(); App
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Windows Forms entry point (Main method with Application.Run) should be marked with [STAThread] attribute.

logic.reasoning: Windows Forms requires Single-Threaded Apartment (STA) threading model. The Main method must be marked with [STAThread] when using Windows Forms (Application.Run, etc.). Missing this attribute can cause threading issues.

should_trigger: public static void Main() { Application.EnableVisualStyles(); Application.Run(new MainForm()); }
should_not_trigger: [STAThread] public static void Main() { Application.Run(new MainForm()); }
